articles.title: Navigating the Private Credit Yield Landscape in 2025
articles.summary: An overview of yield opportunities and risk premia across private credit segments in the current rate environment.
articles.content: In 2025, private credit continues to offer an attractive yield premium over broadly syndicated loans and high yield bonds. Direct lending strategies, in particular, benefit from tighter covenants and bespoke structuring. This article analyzes current spreads by borrower size, sector, and seniority in the capital structure, and examines how manager selection and structuring discipline influence realized returns.

articles.title: Structured Solutions in Private Credit: Unitranche and Beyond
articles.summary: How unitranche, delayed-draw, and PIK structures are evolving in sponsor-backed deals.
articles.content: Sponsor-backed borrowers increasingly seek flexibility in their capital structures. Unitranche facilities, delayed-draw term loans, and PIK toggles can align incentives between sponsors and lenders while preserving downside protection. We review recent deal structures, key terms, and case studies where bespoke private credit solutions unlocked complex transactions.

articles.title: Three Pillars of Risk Management in Private Credit Portfolios
articles.summary: A framework for underwriting, monitoring, and workout planning in private credit.
articles.content: Effective risk management in private credit starts with disciplined underwriting, but it does not end there. Managers must actively monitor performance covenants, sector fundamentals, and sponsor behavior. This article outlines three pillars of risk management: upfront structuring, proactive portfolio monitoring, and robust workout planning, supported by real-world examples from recent cycles.

articles.title: Covenant Trends in Middle-Market Private Credit
articles.summary: How covenant-light dynamics in syndicated markets influence middle-market private deals.
articles.content: While covenant-lite structures dominate broadly syndicated markets, middle-market private credit still relies on maintenance covenants to manage downside risk. We analyze recent covenant packages across sectors, discuss sponsor pushback on leverage and coverage tests, and highlight where lenders are holding the line.

articles.title: Scaling SaaS with Growth Equity: Lessons from 50+ Investments
articles.summary: Key drivers of value creation in growth-stage SaaS backed by growth equity capital.
articles.content: Growth equity investors in SaaS focus on efficient growth, cohort economics, and capital allocation discipline. Drawing on more than 50 historical investments, we examine which metrics most strongly correlate with outsized outcomes and how investors partner with management teams to accelerate go-to-market strategies.

articles.title: Growth Equity in Healthcare IT: Balancing Innovation and Regulation
articles.summary: Opportunities and pitfalls in backing healthcare IT platforms at the inflection point.
articles.content: Healthcare IT sits at the intersection of innovation and regulation. Growth equity investors must underwrite not only market adoption but also reimbursement, compliance, and data security. We provide a checklist for evaluating healthcare IT deals, from product-market fit to go-to-market models.

articles.title: Operational Value Creation in Mid-Market Buyouts
articles.summary: Why operational levers often matter more than multiple expansion in mid-market buyouts.
articles.content: In the current environment, underwriting buyouts to multiple expansion alone is no longer sufficient. We explore operational value-creation levers such as pricing, procurement, footprint optimization, and digital transformation, with case studies from industrial and business services deals.

articles.title: Partnering with Founder-Led Businesses in Buyout Transactions
articles.summary: Best practices for structuring and governing buyouts of family- and founder-owned companies.
articles.content: Founder and family owners have distinct objectives and concerns in buyout transactions. We discuss tools such as earn-outs, rollover equity, and governance frameworks that align incentives and preserve culture while enabling institutional value creation.

articles.title: Infrastructure Investing in the Energy Transition
articles.summary: Where private capital is finding risk-adjusted returns in the energy transition.
articles.content: The energy transition is reshaping infrastructure investing across power generation, grid modernization, EV charging, and distributed resources. We discuss how contractual frameworks and regulatory regimes shape risk/return profiles for private infrastructure investors.

articles.title: What the 2025 Rate Cycle Means for Private Markets
articles.summary: Implications of the evolving rate environment for deal activity and valuations.
articles.content: Central banks have shifted from rapid tightening to a more data-dependent posture. We examine how changes in base rates and credit spreads are influencing private equity, growth equity, and private credit deal volumes and pricing.

articles.title: Quarterly Market Insights: Q4 2025
articles.summary: A cross-strategy review of fundraising, deployment, and exit markets in Q4 2025.
articles.content: Our Q4 2025 Market Insights highlight key trends across strategies, including stabilizing valuations in growth equity, resilient demand for private credit, and selective multiple expansion in high-quality buyouts.

articles.title: Firm Opens New York Office to Support North American Growth
articles.summary: Expansion of our North American presence with a new office in New York.
articles.content: To better serve our portfolio companies and investors in North America, we have opened a new office in New York. The office will house investment professionals across buyout, growth equity, and private credit.

team_members.biography: Julia is a Partner in the North America team and leads technology investing, with a focus on vertical SaaS and data platforms. She joined the firm in 2014 and has led over 20 growth and buyout transactions.
Michael is a Partner in the European private credit strategy, specializing in unitranche and second-lien solutions for sponsor-backed borrowers.
Sarah is a Managing Director in the Growth Equity team, focusing on healthcare IT and software businesses.
Robert leads the European mid-market buyout strategy, with a focus on industrial and business services companies.
Laura is a Director in the private credit team, responsible for underwriting and portfolio management in DACH and Benelux.
Daniel is a Vice President in the Private Credit team, focusing on unitranche and mezzanine financings for sponsor-backed companies.
Helen is the firm’s Senior Economic Advisor, providing macroeconomic and market insights to investment teams and clients.
Anthony leads the Infrastructure Equity strategy, with a focus on energy transition and digital infrastructure assets.
Emily is a Principal in the North America private credit team, focusing on industrial and infrastructure-related credits.
James is an Operating Partner supporting value creation initiatives in healthcare and business services portfolio companies.
The Research Team supports investment and client teams with data-driven insights across strategies and regions.
Amir is an Analyst supporting the European buyout team in industrial and business services sectors.
Janet manages investor relations and reporting for Asia-Pacific clients.
